Operations were performed on South Pelto 5, OCS-G 12027, Well B-1ST1 to reduce sand production and restore production from the "U-1" Sand.  A TTGP was installed above the "U-1" Sand perforations, 13838' - 13853' MD, to reduce sand production from the completion interval.  The well was then jetted with nitrogen to restore production from the "U-1" Sand.  Operations began on June 4, 2010 and were completed on July 29, 2010.  Operations in restoring production from the "U-1" Sand were unsuccessful.
Well was temporarily abandoned on 10/16/2010.

Stone has successfully completed zone change operations on the SS 112 T-2 well to produce the "R-4" Sand. The previous SS completion, the "R-11" sand, and the "R-13A" LS completion was isolated by cutting & pulling the 2-3/8" LS tbg at 10,005' MD and the 2-3/8" SS tbg at 10,010' MD. An EZSV cement retainer was set int he 7-5/8" csg at 9,600' MD and 25 bbls of cement was squeezed into the perforations of the "R-11" and "R-13A" sands. The "R-4" Sand was perforated at intervals 9,522'-9,532' MD and a new 2-7/8" tbg was installed. Operations began 04/29/11 and ended 06/01/11.
